DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR FAILURE TO STATE A CLAIM RE: THE CONVERSION OF AN IDEA. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED FACTS SHOWING THE CLAIM IS NOT PREEMPTED BY STATE AND FEDERAL INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY LAW OR THAT THE PROPERTY CONVERTED WAS TANGIBLE PROPERTY. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECT CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Demurrer To The First Amended Complaint For Failure To State A Claim Re: The Conversion Of An Idea [Redacted]
Off Calendar Nov-12-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS BNY MELLON PERFORMANCE & RISK ANALYTICS, INC., THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON, MELLON CAPITAL MANAGEMENT CORP., MELLON FINANCIAL SERVICES CORP. #1, MELLON LEASING CORP., MELLON VENTURES, INC., MBSC SECURIITES CORP., AND URDANG CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, INC. Demurrer to the First Amended Complaint for Failure to State a Claim Re: The Conversion of an Idea. Appearances: John S. Karls, (917) 270-1280, Plaintiff in Pro Per appearing and opposing party; Peter Obstler, Esq. and Marjory Gentry, Esq. of Bingham McCutchen LLP 415) 393-2790, appearing for Defendants and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff has not alleged facts showing the claim is not preempted by state and federal intellectual property law or that the property converted was tangible property. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-14-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BNY MELLON PERFORMANCE & RISK ANALYTICS, INC.; MELLON CAPITAL MANAGEMENT CORPORATION; MELLON FINANCIAL SERVICES CORPORATION #1; MELLON LEASING CORPORATION; MELLON VENTURES, INC., MBSC SECURITIES CORPORATION, URDANG CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, INC., and, THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: Marjory Gentry, Esq. and Peter Obstler, Esq. of Bingham McCutchen LLP 415) 393-2790, appearing for Defendant and moving party; John Karls, Plaintiff Pro Per and opposing party appearing (917) 270-1280. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. It is unclear that prior court orders bar this action. Plaintiff must plead facts that demonstrate his claim is not preempted under California and federal law and that his conversion claim is for tangible property. (Melchior vs. Newline Productions (2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 779.) Order signed in open Court sustaining the Bank of New York Mellon's Demurrer for failure to state a claim and granting motion to dismiss non-existing party.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Mary Ellen Scherer, CSR #5208

Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Under Seal Portions Of Reply In Support Of Demurrer For Failure To State A Claim, And Demurrer Or, In The Alternative, Non-Statuary Motion To Quash Service; The Transcript Of The Depposition Of John S. Karls,

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BNY MELLON PERFORMANCE & RISK ANALYTICS, INC., MELLON CAPITAL MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, and, THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON Motion to File Under Seal Portions of Reply in Support of Demurrer for Failure to State a Claim, and Demurrer or, In The Alternative, Non-Statuary Motion to Quash Service. Appearances: Marjory Gentry, Esq. and Peter Obstler, Esq. of Bingham McCutchen LLP 415) 393-2790, appearing for Defendant and moving party;
John Karls, Plaintiff Pro Per and opposing party appearing (917) 270-1280. This tentative ruling is not being contested. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied without prejudice. Defendants to comply with CRC 2.550 and 2.551. Originally lodged sealed documents: The bank of New York Mellon Defendants reply in support of Demurrer for failure to state a claim re: plaintiff's complaint for conversion of an idea; and (2) Demurrer or in the alternative, non statutory motion to quash Service and dismiss defendant the Bank of New York U.S. Tax consolidated Group: of corporations for lack of jurisdiction returned to counsel at hearing. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Mary Ellen Scherer, CSR #5208

Off Calendar Sep-14-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BNY MELLON PERFORMANCE & RISK ANALYTICS, INC.; MELLON CAPITAL MANAGEMENT CORPORATION; MELLON FINANCIAL SERVICES CORPORATION #1; MELLON LEASING CORPORATION; MELLON VENTURES, INC.; MBSC SECURITIES CORPORATION; URDANG CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, INC.; and, THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON Motion To Quash Service and Dismiss Defendant the Bank of New York U.S. Tax Consolidated Group of Corporations. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to quash and dismiss granted. Suit is brought against an entity that does not legally exist.
